enforced.oracle: Canonical JSON representation must be identical after reordering meta-information lines (except ##fileformat which must remain first)

evidence.quote: Other than |##fileformat|, they may appear in any order.

enforced.oracle: Canonical JSON representation must be identical after reordering key=value pairs inside ##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER structured lines

evidence.quote: Implementations must not rely on the order of the fields within structured lines and are not required to preserve field ordering.

enforced.oracle: Records with POS=0 or POS=N+1 (telomere positions) must preserve their coordinate values after round-trip conversion between VCF and BCF formats

evidence.quote:
* POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
  Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM.
  It is permitted to have multiple records with the same POS.
  Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig.
  (Integer, Required)

* POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
  Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM.
  It is permitted to have multiple records with the same POS.
  Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig.
  (Integer, Required)

enforced.oracle: POS field values must remain unchanged after parse-write-parse round-trip, preserving the 1-based coordinate system where position 1 corresponds to the first reference base

evidence.quote:
* POS — position: The reference position, with the 1st base having position 1.
  Positions are sorted numerically, in increasing order, within each reference sequence CHROM.
  It is permitted to have multiple records with the same POS.
  Telomeres are indicated by using positions 0 or N+1, where N is the length of the corresponding chromosome or contig.
  (Integer, Required)

This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of:
the position of the final base of the REF allele,
the end position corresponding to the SVLEN of a symbolic SV allele,
and the end positions calculated from FORMAT LEN for the $<$*$>$ symbolic allele.

enforced.oracle: When left-aligning an indel in a homopolymer context, the new POS must correctly reference the padding base position according to 1-based coordinates

evidence.quote: For simple insertions and deletions in which either the REF or one of the ALT alleles would otherwise be null/empty, the REF and ALT Strings must include the base before the variant (which must be reflected in the POS field), unless the variant occurs at position 1 on the contig in which case it must include the base after the variant

enforced.oracle: END field values (when present) must be preserved as 1-based coordinates through parse-write-parse round-trip

evidence.quote:
This is a computed field that, when present, must be set to the maximum end reference position (1-based) of:
the position of the final base of the REF allele

enforced.oracle: VCF → BCF → VCF round-trip must produce identical canonical content as original VCF

evidence.quote:
Overall, the idea behind is BCF2 is simple.
BCF2 is a binary, compressed equivalent of VCF that can be indexed with tabix and can be efficiently decoded from disk or streams.
For efficiency reasons BCF2 only supports a subset of VCF, in that all info and genotype fields must have their full types specified.
That is, BCF2 requires that if e.g. an info field AC is present then it must contain an equivalent VCF header line noting that AC is an allele indexed array of type integer.

quarantine.mr_name: ALT allele permutation with consistent field remapping
quarantine.oracle: The canonical JSON representation after permutation must be identical to the original (up to ALT order and corresponding field reordering)

evidence.quote: AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
AF		    A		    Float		    Allele frequency for each ALT allele in the same order as listed (estimated from primary data, not called genotypes)
AC		    A		    Integer	    Allele count in genotypes, for each ALT allele, in the same order as listed
AF		    A		    Float		    Allele frequency for each ALT allele in the same order as listed (estimated from primary data, not called genotypes)

quarantine.oracle: The canonical JSON representation of the variant must be identical after trimming shared prefix/suffix bases to produce parsimonious left-aligned representation

quarantine.oracle: A VCF parser must reject any record that is missing one or more of the mandatory fixed fields (#CHROM, POS, ID, REF, ALT, QUAL, FILTER, INFO)

evidence.quote: #CHROM     POS     ID     REF     ALT     QUAL     FILTER     INFO
#CHROM     POS     ID     REF     ALT     QUAL     FILTER     INFO
#CHROM     POS     ID     REF     ALT     QUAL     FILTER     INFO

quarantine.mr_name: BCF header dictionary order permutation invariance
quarantine.oracle: Shuffling ##contig/##INFO/##FORMAT/##FILTER lines and round-tripping through BCF must yield identical canonical content

evidence.quote: Defined this way, the dictionary of strings depends on the order and the presence of all preceding header lines.
It is recommended to respect the ordering as specified in the input VCF/BCF2 file, but parsers should not rely on a specific ordering.

ambiguity_flags: The spec says 'parsers should not rely on a specific ordering' (ADVISORY) but the dictionary depends on order (CRITICAL)

quarantine.oracle: The set of variants represented by splitting a multi-ALT record into separate records must be semantically equivalent to the original multi-ALT representation

evidence.quote: LAA		    .			    Integer	    1-based indices into ALT, indicating which alleles are relevant (local) for the current sample
* ALT — alternate base(s): Comma-separated list of alternate non-reference alleles.

quarantine.oracle: Canonical JSON output must be identical after reordering semicolon-separated key=value entries in INFO column

evidence.quote: INFO — additional information: Semicolon-separated series of additional information fields

ambiguity_flags: The spec doesn't explicitly state INFO field order is insignificant, but semicolon-separated series implies unordered collection
